verses.latin_vulgate: Verba Lamuelis regis. Visio qua erudivit eum mater sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּ֭בְרֵי לְמוּאֵ֣ל מֶ֑לֶךְ מַ֝שָּׂ֗א אֲֽשֶׁר־יִסְּרַ֥תּוּ אִמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid, dilecte mi ? quid, dilecte uteri mei ?  quid, dilecte votorum meorum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־בְּ֭רִי וּמַֽה־בַּר־בִּטְנִ֑י וּ֝מֶ֗ה בַּר־נְדָרָֽי׃
verses.king_james: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? and what, the son of my vows?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne dederis mulieribus substantiam tuam,  et divitias tuas ad delendos reges.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּתֵּ֣ן לַנָּשִׁ֣ים חֵילֶ֑ךָ וּ֝דְרָכֶ֗יךָ לַֽמְח֥וֹת מְלָכִֽין׃
verses.king_james: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to that which destroyeth kings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli regibus, o Lamuel, noli regibus dare vinum,  quia nullum secretum est ubi regnat ebrietas ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֤ל לַֽמְלָכִ֨ים ׀ לְֽמוֹאֵ֗ל אַ֣ל לַֽמְלָכִ֣ים שְׁתוֹ־יָ֑יִן וּ֝לְרוֹזְנִ֗ים (או) [אֵ֣י] שֵׁכָֽר׃
verses.king_james: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink:

verses.latin_vulgate: et ne forte bibant, et obliviscantur judiciorum,  et mutent causam filiorum pauperis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּן־יִ֭שְׁתֶּה וְיִשְׁכַּ֣ח מְחֻקָּ֑ק וִ֝ישַׁנֶּ֗ה דִּ֣ין כׇּל־בְּנֵי־עֹֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Date siceram mœrentibus,  et vinum his qui amaro sunt animo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּנוּ־שֵׁכָ֥ר לְאוֹבֵ֑ד וְ֝יַ֗יִן לְמָ֣רֵי נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto those that be of heavy hearts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bibant, et obliviscantur egestatis suæ,  et doloris sui non recordentur amplius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִ֭שְׁתֶּה וְיִשְׁכַּ֣ח רִישׁ֑וֹ וַ֝עֲמָל֗וֹ לֹ֣א יִזְכׇּר־עֽוֹד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperi os tuum muto,  et causis omnium filiorum qui pertranseunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּתַח־פִּ֥יךָ לְאִלֵּ֑ם אֶל־דִּ֝֗ין כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י חֲלֽוֹף׃
verses.king_james: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperi os tuum, decerne quod justum est,  et judica inopem et pauperem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּתַח־פִּ֥יךָ שְׁפׇט־צֶ֑דֶק וְ֝דִ֗ין עָנִ֥י וְאֶבְיֽוֹן׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulierem fortem quis inveniet ?  procul et de ultimis finibus pretium ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵֽשֶׁת־חַ֭יִל מִ֣י יִמְצָ֑א וְרָחֹ֖ק מִפְּנִינִ֣ים מִכְרָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confidit in ea cor viri sui,  et spoliis non indigebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֣טַח בָּ֭הּ לֵ֣ב בַּעְלָ֑הּ וְ֝שָׁלָ֗ל לֹ֣א יֶחְסָֽר׃
verses.king_james: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reddet ei bonum, et non malum,  omnibus diebus vitæ suæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גְּמָלַ֣תְהוּ ט֣וֹב וְלֹא־רָ֑ע כֹּ֝֗ל יְמֵ֣י חַיֶּֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæsivit lanam et linum,  et operata est consilia manuum suarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דָּ֭רְשָׁה צֶ֣מֶר וּפִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַ֝תַּ֗עַשׂ בְּחֵ֣פֶץ כַּפֶּֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta est quasi navis institoris,  de longe portans panem suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָ֭יְתָה כׇּאֳנִיּ֣וֹת סוֹחֵ֑ר מִ֝מֶּרְחָ֗ק תָּבִ֥יא לַחְמָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et de nocte surrexit,  deditque prædam domesticis suis,  et cibaria ancillis suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּ֤קׇם ׀ בְּע֬וֹד לַ֗יְלָה וַתִּתֵּ֣ן טֶ֣רֶף לְבֵיתָ֑הּ וְ֝חֹ֗ק לְנַעֲרֹתֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consideravit agrum, et emit eum ;  de fructu manuum suarum plantavit vineam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זָמְמָ֣ה שָׂ֭דֶה וַתִּקָּחֵ֑הוּ מִפְּרִ֥י כַ֝פֶּ֗יהָ (נטע) [נָ֣טְעָה] כָּֽרֶם׃
verses.king_james: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accinxit fortitudine lumbos suos,  et roboravit brachium suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָֽגְרָ֣ה בְע֣וֹז מׇתְנֶ֑יהָ וַ֝תְּאַמֵּ֗ץ זְרוֹעֹתֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gustavit, et vidit quia bona est negotiatio ejus ;  non extinguetur in nocte lucerna ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טָ֭עֲמָה כִּי־ט֣וֹב סַחְרָ֑הּ לֹא־יִכְבֶּ֖ה (בליל) [בַלַּ֣יְלָה] נֵרָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: She perceiveth that her merchandise is good: her candle goeth not out by night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manum suam misit ad fortia,  et digiti ejus apprehenderunt fusum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָ֭דֶיהָ שִׁלְּחָ֣ה בַכִּישׁ֑וֹר וְ֝כַפֶּ֗יהָ תָּ֣מְכוּ פָֽלֶךְ׃
verses.king_james: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manum suam aperuit inopi,  et palmas suas extendit ad pauperem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּ֭פָּהּ פָּֽרְשָׂ֣ה לֶעָנִ֑י וְ֝יָדֶ֗יהָ שִׁלְּחָ֥ה לָאֶבְיֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non timebit domui suæ a frigoribus nivis ;  omnes enim domestici ejus vestiti sunt duplicibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־תִירָ֣א לְבֵיתָ֣הּ מִשָּׁ֑לֶג כִּ֥י כׇל־בֵּ֝יתָ֗הּ לָבֻ֥שׁ שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household are clothed with scarlet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stragulatam vestem fecit sibi ;  byssus et purpura indumentum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַרְבַדִּ֥ים עָֽשְׂתָה־לָּ֑הּ שֵׁ֖שׁ וְאַרְגָּמָ֣ן לְבוּשָֽׁהּ׃
verses.king_james: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing is silk and purple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nobilis in portis vir ejus,  quando sederit cum senatoribus terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נוֹדָ֣ע בַּשְּׁעָרִ֣ים בַּעְלָ֑הּ בְּ֝שִׁבְתּ֗וֹ עִם־זִקְנֵי־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.king_james: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sindonem fecit, et vendidit,  et cingulum tradidit Chananæo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סָדִ֣ין עָ֭שְׂתָה וַתִּמְכֹּ֑ר וַ֝חֲג֗וֹר נָתְנָ֥ה לַֽכְּנַעֲנִֽי׃
verses.king_james: She maketh fine linen, and selleth it; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fortitudo et decor indumentum ejus,  et ridebit in die novissimo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹז־וְהָדָ֥ר לְבוּשָׁ֑הּ וַ֝תִּשְׂחַ֗ק לְי֣וֹם אַחֲרֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: Strength and honour are her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Os suum aperuit sapientiæ,  et lex clementiæ in lingua ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פִּ֭יהָ פָּתְחָ֣ה בְחׇכְמָ֑ה וְת֥וֹרַת חֶ֝֗סֶד עַל־לְשׁוֹנָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue is the law of kindness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consideravit semitas domus suæ,  et panem otiosa non comedit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צ֭וֹפִיָּה (הילכות) [הֲלִיכ֣וֹת] בֵּיתָ֑הּ וְלֶ֥חֶם עַ֝צְל֗וּת לֹ֣א תֹאכֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexerunt filii ejus, et beatissimam prædicaverunt ;  vir ejus, et laudavit eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָ֣מוּ בָ֭נֶיהָ וַֽיְאַשְּׁר֑וּהָ בַּ֝עְלָ֗הּ וַֽיְהַלְלָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praiseth her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multæ filiæ congregaverunt divitias ;  tu supergressa es universas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רַבּ֣וֹת בָּ֭נוֹת עָ֣שׂוּ חָ֑יִל וְ֝אַ֗תְּ עָלִ֥ית עַל־כֻּלָּֽנָה׃
verses.king_james: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fallax gratia, et vana est pulchritudo :  mulier timens Dominum, ipsa laudabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֶׁ֣קֶר הַ֭חֵן וְהֶ֣בֶל הַיֹּ֑פִי אִשָּׁ֥ה יִרְאַת־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה הִ֣יא תִתְהַלָּֽל׃
verses.king_james: Favour is deceitful, and beauty is vain: but a woman that feareth the Lord, she shall be praised.

verses.latin_vulgate: Date ei de fructu manuum suarum,  et laudent eam in portis opera ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּנוּ־לָ֭הּ מִפְּרִ֣י יָדֶ֑יהָ וִיהַלְל֖וּהָ בַשְּׁעָרִ֣ים מַֽעֲשֶֽׂיהָ׃
verses.king_james: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.
